_readme.what_this_is: An archetype MANIFEST: the durable standard for a class of website, expressed as a selection of checkpoints from the shared library (library/checkpoints/) plus the severity each carries in THIS domain. The library defines WHAT each checkpoint checks (transferable); the manifest assigns HOW MUCH it matters here. Resolve to the full archetype with `node library/resolve.mjs archetype.ecommerce.json`.
_readme.why_a_manifest: Checkpoints recur across site types (auth, money precision, payment webhooks, contrast). Authoring them once in the library and composing per archetype means archetype #2 reuses instead of copies — the moat compounds and a fix to a shared checkpoint improves every archetype at once.

the_walls.pattern_instance: Library + manifest are both PATTERN (transferable). Instance details (a project's real code) are generated at runtime and never written back. Facts about good software are free to encode; specific code is not.
the_walls.strength_blindspot: Checkpoints tag domain:backbone or domain:design. Backbone ENFORCES the builder's own judgment; design SUPPLIES judgment the builder lacks.

_readme.ids_are_contracts: Checkpoint ids (auth.access_control, payment.webhook_authenticity, ecommerce.checkout.atomic_stock_hold) are stable, permanent, and namespaced: shared concerns are domain-neutral, domain-specific ones carry their domain prefix. Calibration history keys to them, so they are versioned, never silently renamed.
_readme.how_scoring_works: Levels 3/6/9. 3 = present but crude/risky. 6 = solid, shippable. 9 = great. Verifier reports level + concrete gap to next level. TRUST THE GAP, NOT THE NUMBER. Goal: 9 on critical, 6+ elsewhere - never infinite polish.
_readme.instrumented_design_layer: Three design checkpoints have a richer, instrumented definition in archetype.ecommerce.design.json (weighted sub-signals -> a computed 0-10 composite): design.visual_hierarchy and ecommerce.design.trust_signals (authoritative there when it runs; the library definition is the fallback), plus design.aesthetic_coherence which measures the RENDERED result, complementing the static token checks in design.system_consistency and design.spacing_system. See that file's _readme.relationship_to_base_archetype for the 0-10 -> 3/6/9 mapping.

applies_when: The site takes money for goods/services: a catalog, a cart or equivalent, and a payment flow.

goal_definition.honest_ceiling: design checkpoints tagged taste_limited verify to ~level 7 mechanically; reaching 9 requires human design review the rubric cannot replace.
